doc.title: Funded Status on the Balance Sheet
doc.event: At December 31, Ingle Co.'s pension plan reports a projected benefit obligation of $1,200,000. Plan assets at fair value differ from the PBO. Determine the amount and classification Ingle reports on its balance sheet.

computationPaths.narration: Plan assets $950,000 vs PBO $1,200,000. The balance sheet shows the NET position only — a single Pension Liability.
Plan assets $1,300,000 vs PBO $1,200,000. Overfunded plans show a single Pension Asset.

memorize.body: Funded status = Plan assets (FV) − PBO. Negative → net pension liability; positive → net pension asset.
Report the NET number only. PBO and plan assets never appear gross on the balance sheet — the detail lives in the notes.

questions.prompt: PBO $1,200,000, plan assets $950,000. What amount appears on Ingle's balance sheet?

distractors.feedback: You reported the gross PBO. The balance sheet shows the NET of assets against the obligation: 1,200,000 − 950,000 = 250,000 liability.
That's the gross plan assets. Net the two: 250,000 pension liability.
Pensions are NOT off-balance-sheet under current GAAP — the funded status must be reported.
